perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Without derogating from CBC's representations and warranties herein, CBC and MediWound shall work together in order to enable the CBC facility to accomplish all required standards, related to the manufacturing, packaging and delivering of Bromelain SP in accordance with the Specifications, GACP (Good Agricultural and Collection Practice) and cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) standards, ISO 22000 and all other applicable laws and regulations. For such purpose, and without derogating from other terms herein, CBC shall permit MediWound, and/or a consultant on MediWound's behalf, to access and inspect the CBC facility and advise MediWound and/or CBC on such actions to be taken for accomplishing such compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Without derogating from CBC's representations and warranties herein, CBC and MediWound shall endeavor to work together in order to enable the CBC facility to meet standards, related to the manufacturing, packaging and delivering of Bromelain SP in general accordance with the Specifications, GACP (Good Agricultural and Collection Practice) and cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) standards, ISO 22000 and all other applicable laws and regulations. For such purpose, CBC may permit MediWound, and/or a consultant on MediWound's behalf, to access and inspect the CBC facility and offer advice on actions to be considered for such compliance.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires CBC to work with MediWound to accomplish all required standards. The modified text weakens this obligation by stating that CBC and MediWound shall 'endeavor' to work together and that CBC shall only meet standards in 'general accordance' with regulations. The modification also changes 'shall permit' to 'may permit', creating ambiguity on if access will even be granted. Finally, the text softens the obligation to take actions to be taken to 'actions to be considered'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR Part 211 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice Regulations
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to adhere to cGMP standards. 21 CFR § 211.22 mandates strict written procedures for production and process controls. The modified text, using terms like 'endeavor' and 'general accordance', suggests a more relaxed approach, potentially conflicting with the strict requirements for quality control procedures in pharmaceutical manufacturing mandated by the FDA and cGMP.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the original obligations. The original contract required CBC to 'accomplish all required standards,' whereas the modified text states that CBC and Mediwound 'endeavor to work together' to 'meet standards' in 'general accordance' with regulations, and 'may permit' MediWound access, which weakens the initial obligation. The Law Explanation, citing 21 CFR § 211.22, highlights the need for strict written procedures, while the Perturbation Explanation notes the use of 'endeavor' and 'general accordance' as creating a relaxed approach. The scraped law snippet 1 cites 21 CFR 211.22 regarding Responsibilities of quality control unit. This demonstrates that the change could create a legal contradiction related to quality control and cGMP standards.

perturbation.original_text: 5.4 CBC undertakes to keep all records reasonably required by MediWound relating to the manufacture, quality control and testing of Bromelain SP. Such records shall include, but not be limited to, all records required by applicable laws and regulations, of the territories in which the Product is marketed and sold. MediWound (itself or through anyone on its behalf) or any relevant regulatory authority shall have the right to audit any such records and/or the relevant facilities of CBC (or any facilities of any CBC third party or subcontractor involved in the manufacture, quality control and/or supply of the Bromelain SP) with reasonable prior notice, during regular business hours, including the right to ask CBC to provide any relevant documents.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 CBC will attempt to keep records relating to the manufacture, quality control and testing of Bromelain SP. Records may include those required by applicable laws and regulations, of the territories in which the Product is marketed and sold. MediWound (itself or through anyone on its behalf) or any relevant regulatory authority may be allowed to audit such records and/or the relevant facilities of CBC (or any facilities of any CBC third party or subcontractor involved in the manufacture, quality control and/or supply of the Bromelain SP) with notification, during possible business hours, with the right to request CBC to provide relevant documents.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates CBC to keep all records and grants MediWound the right to audit the facility. The modified text changes 'undertakes to keep all records' to 'will attempt to keep records,' weakening the obligation. 'Shall have the right to audit' is changed to 'may be allowed to audit,' making the audit discretionary. 'Reasonable prior notice' is changed to just 'notification', removing clarity on the duration required for the notice. 'Regular business hours' is changed to 'possible business hours', also impacting the duration of the audit. This impacts record-keeping and audit compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modifications weaken the record-keeping and audit requirements. 21 CFR § 211.180 outlines specific requirements for maintaining accurate and complete records of drug product manufacturing. Changing 'undertakes to keep all records' to 'will attempt to keep records' and making the audit discretionary ('may be allowed') directly contradicts the mandatory record-keeping and audit trail requirements essential for GMP compliance.

perturbation.justification: The modifications weaken the mandatory record-keeping and audit requirements, creating a contradiction. The original text mandates specific actions, while the modified text introduces ambiguity and discretion, potentially leading to non-compliance. The Law Explanation states that changing 'undertakes to keep all records' to 'will attempt to keep records' and making the audit discretionary ('may be allowed') directly contradicts the mandatory record-keeping requirements. The snippet mentions 21 CFR 211.180 outlining requirements for maintaining accurate and complete records, which supports that the original text's stronger obligation is legally relevant, and the weakening introduces a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.8.2 In the event that CBC has any reason to believe that the Bromelain SP or one or more Products should be recalled or withdrawn from distribution, CBC shall immediately notify MediWound in writing. In such event MediWound shall, at MediWound's sole discretion, determine whether to recall or withdraw the Product from the market.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.8.2 In the event that CBC has a reason to consider that the Bromelain SP or one or more Products might need to be recalled or withdrawn from distribution, CBC should endeavor to notify MediWound. In such event MediWound can, at its discretion, consider whether to recall or withdraw the Product from the market.
perturbation.explanation: Original text says 'CBC shall immediately notify MediWound in writing', which has been changed to 'CBC should endeavor to notify MediWound.' Also, 'has any reason to believe' is modified to 'reason to consider' and 'should be recalled' to 'might need to be recalled'. This significantly weakens the responsibility of CBC to communicate the need for a recall, hindering patient safety.
perturbation.law_explanation: By changing "shall immediately notify" to "should endeavor to notify," the modified text undermines the prompt reporting of potential hazards. 21 CFR § 7.41 requires firms to report potential recalls to the FDA promptly. This change creates ambiguity around the urgency and obligation for reporting, conflicting with regulatory requirements aimed at swift action to protect public health.

perturbation.justification: The modified text changes "shall immediately notify" to "should endeavor to notify," weakening the obligation to report potential recalls. This contradicts the intent of regulations like 21 CFR § 7.41, which emphasizes prompt reporting of potential hazards. The scraped snippet, referencing 21 CFR 7.41, supports this by highlighting the importance of health hazard evaluation and recall classification, implying the need for immediate action, which the modified text undermines.
